flashcards.question: Define: Kinematics
flashcards.answer: The branch of mechanics that describes the motion of objects without considering the forces that cause the motion

flashcards.question: Define: Force
flashcards.answer: A push or pull that can change an object's velocity, measured in newtons

flashcards.question: Define: Acceleration
flashcards.answer: The rate of change of velocity with respect to time

flashcards.question: Define: Momentum
flashcards.answer: The product of an object's mass and velocity, representing its resistance to motion changes

flashcards.question: Define: Work
flashcards.answer: The transfer of energy that occurs when a force acts on an object over a distance

flashcards.question: Define: Power
flashcards.answer: The rate at which work is done or energy is transferred, measured in watts

flashcards.question: Define: Torque
flashcards.answer: The rotational equivalent of force; a measure of how much a force causes an object to rotate about an axis

flashcards.question: Define: Angular Momentum
flashcards.answer: The product of an object's moment of inertia and its angular velocity

flashcards.question: A projectile is launched horizontally from a cliff. Ignoring air resistance, what is true about its
flashcards.answer: Horizontal velocity remains constant; vertical velocity increases due to gravity

flashcards.question: An object moving in a circle at constant speed has zero acceleration. Is this statement true or fals
flashcards.answer: False; acceleration is directed toward the center (centripetal acceleration)
